title: Warhammer Quest - Take a Look - Part 16 - Littlemonk's brick of cards
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KOedI8Dr__Y

Daniel (@dungeondive) returns to Warhammer Quest to showcase an extraordinary purchase — over 800 professionally printed cards created and sold by fan champion Littlemonk through his Facebook page in a limited run at roughly $175.

The collection is staggering in scope. It includes cards for Littlemonk's Hall of the Hag Queen expansion, the Realm of Savage Orcs (with 20 new dungeon cards and outdoor forest tiles), the Carnival of Carnage (a dark circus-themed expansion with 22 unique treasures like the Strong Man's Mallet), and the complete Ultimate Adventure Book card set. Every official treasure pack (1-3) has been reprinted with corrected rules, balanced gold values, consistent warrior restrictions, and added artwork where originals had none. Littlemonk also created treasure packs 4-6 with entirely custom content.

The cards match the original Warhammer Quest card size exactly — a feat Daniel marvels at given that modern publishers often can't maintain consistency between their own expansions. Each card now features uniform background colours, source identification stamps, and artwork on every single treasure card. The set also includes the Sister of Sigmar character cards, Bounty Hunter expansion with 72 outlaw tokens, White Dwarf event and monster cards, and Dark Secret cards with upgraded readability. Daniel views it as an investment in both his collection and his time.

----

Would you invest in premium fan-made content for a game you love, or does that cross a line?
